description: Common Palantir Foundry ontology design patterns and best practices

relationship_patterns.association: Entity relatedTo Entity (many_to_many via link table)

best_practices: Every object type should have a human-readable title_property
Use surrogate keys (auto-increment ID) as primary keys, with natural keys as indexed unique fields
Include audit fields (created_at, updated_at) on every object type
Model state as a lifecycle with explicit transitions, not just an enum field
Prefer many_to_one over many_to_many (use link objects for M:N with attributes)
Actions should have explicit preconditions to prevent invalid state transitions
Row-level security filters should reference user attributes, not hardcoded values
Validation rules should have clear error messages suitable for end users
Properties that are computed/derived should be marked as read-only in actions
